summary: Patrick McKenzie argues that email is the most effective broadcast channel for business purposes, yet most geeks and software developers dramatically underutilize it due to misguided psychological blocks. Drawing on his own experience launching a mailing list and six years of blogging, he demonstrates that email vastly outperforms blog posts and RSS feeds in open rates, click-through rates, and actual behavior change. He shows how email keeps leads warm, builds trust through drip campaigns, and can extract meaningful revenue from existing customers. The post doubles as both a persuasive essay and a launch announcement for his own training email list.
key_points: Email open rates (60%) and click-through rates (15%) dramatically outperform blog post engagement, where links get maybe 1-3% clicks
Content presentation shapes perceived value — calling something a 'free PDF' versus a 'best-selling business manifesto' changes how people evaluate identical content
Drip email campaigns can scale the high-touch sales funnel while building trust, as demonstrated by the WPEngine campaign that educated prospects over eight emails
People are terrible at introspecting about their own behavior with email — most geeks claim to hate marketing email while demonstrably responding to it
Email keeps leads warm over time, counteracting the sharp decay in prospect value that occurs without regular contact
money_quotes: Email is the best broadcast channel ever invented for business purposes.
Reading and responding to email is my effing job.
Blog posts are very poor at changing actual behavior.
If you pay nothing for expert advice you will value it at epsilon more than nothing, if you pay five figures for it you will clear your schedule and implement recommendations within the day.
People are statistically speaking terrible at introspection.
Almost every first-class e-commerce company treats their house email campaigns like they are the goose that lays the golden eggs, chiefly because they are.
The probable value of a prospect goes down over time, the provable value of a customer goes up over time.
key_insight: Email massively outperforms blogs, social media, and RSS for driving actual behavior change and revenue because it lands in a workflow people are already committed to reading, rather than competing with thousands of equivalent-looking items in a feed.
